wine_festivals.name: Fira del Vi de Falset
wine_festivals.duration: 3 days (Friday to Sunday, with a professional day on Monday)
wine_festivals.description: The Fira del Vi de Falset is the main annual wine event for the DOQ Priorat and DO Montsant designations, held each year around the first weekend of May in Falset. Around 60 producers pour their current releases across the fairground in Falset, with a separate professional tasting day at Castell de Falset. The fair has run annually for over 30 editions and draws around 30,000 visitors each year.
wine_festivals.tip: Advance tickets sell out. Buy on firadelvi.org before arriving. The professional Monday session at the castle requires trade credentials.

wine_festivals.name: Festa de la Verema a l'Antiga de Poboleda
wine_festivals.description: Held each second Saturday of September in the village of Poboleda, this harvest festival recreates traditional pre-mechanisation grape picking and pressing. Participants use hand tools and stone lagares in a living demonstration of the viticultural methods the Carthusian monks and early modern farmers employed. Over 14 editions have taken place, drawing visitors from across Catalonia seeking an authentic harvest experience in a Priorat village.
wine_festivals.tip: Arrive early to secure a spot in the harvesting demonstration. The village fills by mid-morning. Parking is on the approach road from Cornudella.

wine_festivals.name: Festes de Sant Antoni Abat
wine_festivals.description: The feast of Sant Antoni Abat, patron saint of rural communities and animals, is observed on 17 January across Priorat villages including Poboleda, Torroja del Priorat, and la Vilella Alta. Traditional celebrations include the blessing of animals in the village square, communal meals with local Garnacha wine, and sardanes folk dancing. The festival has deep roots in Catalan rural culture and remains a genuinely local celebration rather than a wine tourism event.
wine_festivals.tip: Sant Antoni is a community festival, not a tourist event. Visitors who arrive respectfully and participate in communal meals will find it the most authentic glimpse of rural Priorat culture.

wine_festivals.name: DOQ Priorat Open Doors Weekend
wine_festivals.host_organization: Consell Regulador de la DOQ Priorat / Priorat Enoturisme
wine_festivals.description: Annual open-cellar weekend across the twelve Vi de Vila villages, with guided tastings and vineyard walks at estates normally closed to walk-in visitors, coordinated by Priorat Enoturisme.
wine_festivals.tip: A single ticket covers all participating estates. Route planning is essential given the distances and driving times between villages on mountain roads.

wine_festivals.name: Festa del Vi Novell
wine_festivals.host_organization: Local cooperatives and Ajuntament de Falset
wine_festivals.description: Each November, Falset and surrounding villages celebrate the release of vi novell, the new wine from the just-completed harvest. The Catalan equivalent of a new-wine celebration, vi novell is poured at cooperative cellars and the wine bars of the Carrer de la Bassa in Falset for a limited window. Cooperatives including Falset-Marca and Vinicola del Priorat open their doors to receive the new harvest.
wine_festivals.tip: Vi novell is available for only a few weeks after the harvest. The cooperatives in Falset are the best source.

wine_festivals.name: Tast Maridatge Priorat
wine_festivals.description: The Tast Maridatge Priorat is a food and wine pairing event held each autumn in Falset that pairs current-release DOQ Priorat wines with Catalan cuisine prepared by local restaurateurs. The format matches specific wines to dishes from the region, with producers on hand to discuss their wines. The event emphasises the traditional Catalan food culture of the comarca alongside the wines.
wine_festivals.tip: Ticket numbers are limited. Book through Priorat Enoturisme in advance. Dishes include romesco-based preparations and calcots when in season.

wine_festivals.name: Wine Heritage Day at Cartoixa d'Escaladei
wine_festivals.recurrence_pattern: annual, varies (typically spring or early summer)
wine_festivals.host_organization: Generalitat de Catalunya / local heritage associations
wine_festivals.description: The ruins of Cartoixa d'Escaladei, the 1163 Carthusian monastery that gave Priorat its name, host an annual heritage day each spring that includes guided tours of the monastery ruins, demonstrations of traditional winemaking, and tastings of Garnacha from the surrounding Escaladei Vi de Vila zone. The monastery ruins are a protected heritage site and the event draws visitors interested in the origin story of the region.
wine_festivals.tip: The Cartoixa ruins are signposted from the village of Escaladei, about 3km down a narrow road from La Morera de Montsant. The site can be visited independently year-round.

wine_festivals.name: Inter-Priorat Annual Trade Tasting
wine_festivals.host_organization: Consell Regulador de la DOQ Priorat
wine_festivals.description: The Consell Regulador de la DOQ Priorat organises an annual trade tasting each spring, presenting the full range of DOQ Priorat producers to importers, sommeliers, and journalists. The tasting showcases wines across the Vi de Vila village classification tiers. Access is restricted to trade and press with accreditation. It is the central showcase for the DOQ to present its wines to the international wine trade.
wine_festivals.tip: Trade attendance requires advance accreditation through the Consell Regulador. Contact doqpriorat.org well ahead of the event date.
